title: Conjecture 7
statement_text: For any fixed integer $c \geq 2$, there exists an integer $t$ such that for infinitely many graphs $H$ with treewidth $t$ and paths $P$, every $c$-colouring of $H \boxtimes P$ has clustering $\Omega(|V(H \boxtimes P)|^{c/(c^2-c+1)})$.
context_text: The upper bound in Theorem 4 gives that $H_1 \boxtimes H_2$ is $c$-colourable with clustering $O(|V(H_1 \boxtimes H_2)|^{c/(c^2-c+1)})$ when one graph has bounded degree. The authors believe this upper bound is asymptotically tight, and furthermore conjecture that there exists a matching construction where the bounded-degree graph is a path.
notes: PDF source — exponents in the clustering bound appear inline without superscript formatting; LaTeX reconstructed from context.

title: Conjecture 8
statement_text: There exists a positive integer $t$ such that for infinitely many graphs $H_1, H_2$ with treewidth $t$, every $3$-colouring of $H_1 \boxtimes H_2$ has clustering $\Omega(|V(H_1 \boxtimes H_2)|^{4/7})$.
context_text: Theorem 3 gives an upper bound of $O(|V(H_1 \boxtimes H_2)|^{4/7})$ for 3-colourings of products of two bounded-treewidth graphs with no degree restriction, but only a lower bound of $\Omega(|V(H_1 \boxtimes H_2)|^{1/2})$ is proved. The authors conjecture the $4/7$ exponent in the upper bound is asymptotically tight.
notes: PDF source — exponent written inline; LaTeX reconstructed from context.

title: Conjecture 9
statement_text: For any fixed integer $c \geq 4$ there exists an integer $k$ such that for infinitely many graphs $H_1, H_2$ with treewidth $k$, every $c$-colouring of $H_1 \boxtimes H_2$ has clustering $\Omega(|V(H_1 \boxtimes H_2)|^{1/((1+o_c(1))\sqrt{c})})$.
context_text: Theorem 6 gives that $H_1 \boxtimes H_2$ is $c$-colourable with clustering $O(|V(H_1 \boxtimes H_2)|^{1/\lfloor\sqrt{c}\rfloor})$ when both graphs have bounded treewidth but no degree restriction. The authors conjecture the upper bound exponent $1/\lfloor\sqrt{c}\rfloor$ is asymptotically tight up to the $o_c(1)$ correction in the exponent denominator.
notes: PDF source — the $o_c(1)$ notation and $\sqrt{c}$ in the exponent are garbled in the raw extraction; LaTeX reconstructed from context. The precise intended statement may differ slightly.
